greet.speaker: npc
greet.text: Well met, traveller. I am Warden Halric, keeper of this isle. Every soul bound for Eldermoor passes my gate first. Shall I teach you the ways of moving and seeing in this world?

options.label: I already know my way around.

where.speaker: npc
where.text: The Wardens' Isle — a quiet shore where the green learn their craft before the mainland. Lose nothing here, and you'll lose less out there.

impatient.speaker: npc
impatient.text: Confidence is well and good, but humour an old warden. Even the seasoned forget a tab or two. It takes but a moment.

controls.speaker: npc
controls.text: To walk, point where you wish to go and your feet will carry you. To look about, drag the view and the world turns around you.

camera.speaker: player
camera.text: And how do I manage my belongings and abilities?

tabs.speaker: npc
tabs.text: Along the side are your tabs — pack, skills, gear, prayers and spells, settings. Tap each to open its panel. One opens as another closes.

settings.speaker: npc
settings.text: The settings panel tunes sound, brightness, and comfort to your liking. Adjust it whenever the journey wears on you.

handoff.speaker: npc
handoff.text: Good. The door behind me is open. Beyond it, find Forester Maeve in the clearing — she'll teach you to live off the land. Off you go.

greet.speaker: npc
greet.text: Ah, one of Halric's new charges. I'm Maeve — I keep folk fed and warm. Before the wilds chew you up, you'll learn to cut wood, kindle fire, net fish, and cook. Ready to begin?

options.label: Why do I need all this?

why.speaker: npc
why.text: A hungry, cold adventurer is a dead one. These four skills are the difference between the road and the crows. Now — woodcutting first.

wood-intro.speaker: npc
wood-intro.text: Here, take this axe. Keep it in your pack — the satchel tab, where all you carry is laid out in little squares.

wood-do.speaker: npc
wood-do.text: Now walk to that tree and put the axe to it. A few good swings yields logs. Come back when your pack holds some.

fire-intro.speaker: npc
fire-intro.text: Logs in hand — well done. Now we make warmth. Take this tinderbox.

options.label: How do I light a fire?

fire-how.speaker: npc
fire-how.text: Use the tinderbox upon your logs and a fire catches on the ground before you. Try it — a steady flame is a friend on cold nights.

fish-intro.speaker: npc
fish-intro.text: A fine blaze. Now to fill our bellies. Here's a fishing net — small holes, good for shrimp.

fish-how.speaker: npc
fish-how.text: Step to the pond's edge where the water shimmers, and cast your net. With patience you'll haul up raw shrimp. Catch a few.

cook-intro.speaker: npc
cook-intro.text: Raw shrimp won't sit well — best cook them. Use a shrimp on your fire and let the heat do the work.

cook-burnt.speaker: npc
cook-burnt.text: Mind you, leave it too long and you'll get a blackened, ruined one. It happens to the best of us — toss the burnt one and cook another. Try it now.

cook-done.speaker: npc
cook-done.text: There — a proper meal. You can live off the land now. Head through the gate to the cottage and speak to Cook Tobin; he'll show you finer fare. Safe travels.

greet.speaker: npc
greet.text: Come in, come in — mind the flour. I'm Tobin, and this is my kitchen. Maeve teaches you to char a shrimp; I'll teach you bread fit for a lord. Interested?

options.label: Show me how to make bread.
Bread? Is that really a skill?

skeptic.speaker: npc
skeptic.text: Scoff if you like, but a baker never starves and never wants for friends. Cooking is cooking, shrimp or fine loaf. Now, to the dough.

dough.speaker: npc
dough.text: Take this pot of flour and a bucket of water. Combine them and knead — that gives a ball of bread dough.

dough-water.speaker: npc
dough-water.text: And here's the water to wet it. A baker's hands are never quite dry.

dough-do.speaker: npc
dough-do.text: Use the water on the flour to make your dough. Then set it on the range yonder to bake into a warm loaf. Go on, make some dough.

done.speaker: npc
done.text: Lovely work — you've a baker's hands. When ready, find Loremaster Edda next door; she keeps the tales and tasks of this world. Off with you.

greet.speaker: npc
greet.text: A new face among the old books. I am Edda, keeper of Eldermoor's lore and its many unfinished tales. Every great deed begins as a quest — would you understand how they work?

books.speaker: npc
books.text: Histories, prophecies, and the troubles of folk who'd pay dearly for a brave soul's help. Those troubles become your quests. Let me explain.

quests.speaker: npc
quests.text: Here, take this journal. Open the Quest tab and you'll see every tale you might take up — some unbegun, some underway, some finished and crowned with reward.

journal.speaker: player
journal.text: So the journal tracks my progress through each story?

journal-yes.speaker: npc
journal-yes.text: Just so. It remembers where you stand and what comes next, so no thread is ever lost. Consult it often and you'll never wander aimless.

done.speaker: npc
done.text: That's the heart of it. Descend the ladder behind me to the mine, where Pickmaster Doran waits. There's iron in your future, I think. Go well.

greet.speaker: npc
greet.text: Mind your head — low ceilings down here. I'm Doran, and these rocks are my trade. Stone to ore, ore to bar, bar to blade. Want the whole chain?

dark.speaker: npc
dark.text: You'll grow used to the dark — there's gold in it, after all. Take this pick and let's see what these veins are hiding.

mine-intro.speaker: npc
mine-intro.text: Here's a pickaxe. See the rocks with pale streaks? That's tin, and that's copper. Swing at them and the ore comes loose.

mine-do.speaker: npc
mine-do.text: Mine yourself a lump of tin ore and a lump of copper ore. You'll need both for what comes next.

smelt-intro.speaker: npc
smelt-intro.text: Tin and copper — good. Alone they're soft and useless. But together, in the furnace, they make bronze.

smelt-how.speaker: npc
smelt-how.text: Take your two ores to that furnace and smelt them. The fire weds them into a single bronze bar. Try it now.

smith-intro.speaker: npc
smith-intro.text: A solid bronze bar. Now we shape it. Take this hammer to the anvil and we'll forge a real tool.

smith-how.speaker: npc
smith-how.text: We'll start simple — a bronze dagger. Use the bar on the anvil and hammer it into shape. Keep the dagger; you'll want it soon.

done.speaker: npc
done.text: A blade from bare rock — that's a smith's pride. Climb the ladder out and find Sergeant Vael in the yard. He'll teach you to swing what you've forged. Good mining.

greet.speaker: npc
greet.text: Stand up straight, recruit. Sergeant Vael, and this is where soft hands learn to fight. You've a dagger — let's make you use it. Ready for combat?

reluctant.speaker: npc
reluctant.text: Out there, things fight you whether you like it or no. Better you learn here, against penned rats, than out there against worse. Now — to your gear.

equip-intro.speaker: npc
equip-intro.text: Open your Equipment tab. A blade in your pack is useless — you must wield it. Here's some leather too; put it on to harden your hide.

equip-gloves.speaker: npc
equip-gloves.text: And gloves — keep a grip on weapon and tool alike. Small things, but they save a life.

equip-do.speaker: npc
equip-do.text: Wield your bronze dagger and wear the leather. Watch your fighter on the field change as you do.

melee-do.speaker: npc
melee-do.text: Now into the pen. Attack a rat and cut it down. You'll earn skill in Attack, Strength, Defence, and the toughness to keep standing. Have at it.

ranged-intro.speaker: npc
ranged-intro.text: Good work up close. But a wise fighter strikes before the enemy reaches him. Take this shortbow.

ranged-ammo.speaker: npc
ranged-ammo.text: And a quiver of arrows to feed it. Each shot spends one, so mind your supply.

ranged-how.speaker: npc
ranged-how.text: Wield the bow with the arrows, then attack a rat from across the pen. Loose a few and drop a rat at range.

styles.speaker: npc
styles.text: Last thing — your Combat tab lets you choose how you fight: accurate, aggressive, defensive, or controlled. Pick what suits the foe. You're soldier enough now.

done.speaker: npc
done.text: Dismissed, recruit — and well done. Through that door is Teller Wynn at the bank. She'll show you where to stow your loot. Move out.

greet.speaker: npc
greet.text: Welcome to the vault, dear. I'm Wynn, and I mind every coin and trinket folk leave in my care. Your pack only holds so much — shall I show you how to bank the rest?

safe.speaker: npc
safe.text: Safe as the stone it's locked behind, and it follows you to every bank in Eldermoor. Nothing here is ever lost, even should you fall. Now, let's open it.

bank-open.speaker: npc
bank-open.text: Step up to the booth and the bank opens. Your stored goods sit in the grid, quite apart from the pack on your back.

bank-use.speaker: npc
bank-use.text: To deposit, place an item in from your pack; to withdraw, draw it back out. Try storing something and taking it back, for the feel of it.

done.speaker: npc
done.text: There — never travel overburdened again. Next, see Steward Brann just through there; he handles your account and the matters of the realm. Good day.

greet.speaker: npc
greet.text: Greetings. I am Brann, steward of these affairs. Duller than swords and dragons, but no less important — your account's safety and your voice in the realm. A moment?

account.speaker: npc
account.text: Take this card — it holds the keys to your wellbeing here. Choose a strong word of passage, trust it to no stranger, and report any trickster who asks for it.

poll-intro.speaker: npc
poll-intro.text: And here's a poll card. See the booth there? When the wardens weigh a change to the realm, every citizen casts a vote. Your word shapes the world — use it.

poll-confirm.speaker: player
poll-confirm.text: So the people decide how Eldermoor grows?

poll-yes.speaker: npc
poll-yes.text: Within reason, and with the wardens' blessing — yes. It keeps the realm honest. Visit the booth whenever a question is put to vote.

done.speaker: npc
done.text: That concludes my business. Step into the chapel beyond and speak with Brother Aldric; he tends matters of the spirit. Go in peace.

greet.speaker: npc
greet.text: Peace be upon you, traveller. I am Brother Aldric, and this humble chapel is a place of rest and reverence. Would you learn the comfort of prayer before you face the world?

options.label: What good is prayer to a fighter?

doubt.speaker: npc
doubt.text: More than you'd think. A whispered prayer can steady a sword arm or turn aside a blow. Faith is armour the eye cannot see. Let me show you.

prayer.speaker: npc
prayer.text: Open your Prayer tab. Each prayer draws on your devotion, replenished at altars such as this. As you grow, more prayers reveal themselves.

bones-intro.speaker: npc
bones-intro.text: Devotion is earned by honouring the fallen. Take these bones, and bury them with respect — the act lends strength to your spirit.

bones-do.speaker: npc
bones-do.text: Use the bones to bury them here in the chapel. Feel your Prayer skill rise. Go on, lay them to rest.

social.speaker: npc
social.text: One more gift — fellowship. Your Friends and Ignore tabs keep good company close and ill company at bay. No adventurer should walk wholly alone.

done.speaker: npc
done.text: Go now with a settled spirit. Beyond the chapel you'll find Magus Sorrel, who wields the arcane arts. Tell her Aldric sent you. Blessings on your road.

greet.speaker: npc
greet.text: Ah — the air shifts when a new pupil arrives. I am Sorrel, and magic is my craft. Steel has its place, but a well-spoken spell ends a fight before it begins. Shall I teach you?

anyone.speaker: npc
anyone.text: Nonsense. The spark lives in every soul brave enough to fan it. Hold these runes and you'll feel it yourself. Now, attend.

magic-tab.speaker: npc
magic-tab.text: Open your Magic tab — there lies your spellbook. Each spell asks for runes, the bottled essence of the elements. Without the right runes, a spell stays grey and silent.

runes-give.speaker: npc
runes-give.text: Air runes, and now mind runes — enough to cast your first spell: Wind Strike. Mind them, for runes are spent with every casting.

cast-do.speaker: npc
cast-do.text: Now choose Wind Strike from your spellbook and aim it at the practice chicken across the room. Speak the word and loose the wind. Try it.

done.speaker: npc
done.text: Marvellous — the spark takes! You've the makings of a mage. Your lessons are done, pupil. Return to Warden Halric at the dock; he'll see you off to the mainland. Fair winds.

greet.speaker: npc
greet.text: Look at you now — chopper, fisher, smith, fighter, and mage. You came to me untested and you leave me ready. There's a boat at the dock bound for the mainland. Will you take it?

options.label: Not yet — anything else I should know?

advice.speaker: npc
advice.text: Only this: trust your training, guard your friends, and never stop learning. Eldermoor is wide and full of wonders. When you're ready, the boat waits.

cape.speaker: npc
cape.text: Then take this cape — let it mark you as one who learned the island's lessons. Wear it with pride.

depart.speaker: npc
depart.text: Now climb aboard, and may the road rise to meet you. Welcome to Eldermoor — your story truly begins now.

greet.speaker: npc
greet.text: Bless you, child. I tend the candles for Brother Aldric. If you've not yet learned the prayers, do speak with him — no shame in a quiet moment of faith before the road.

options.label: How long have you tended this chapel?

years.speaker: npc
years.text: Longer than I'd care to count, dear. I've watched a hundred green adventurers pass through and sail away as heroes. You'll be one of them soon enough.

bye.speaker: npc
bye.text: Go gently. The light keeps for whoever needs it.

greet.speaker: npc
greet.text: Hoy there! Just resting my feet before the crossing. I've done the rounds of this isle twice over — the instructors are a patient lot. Listen to every one, that's my advice.

options.label: Why are you doing it twice?

twice.speaker: npc
twice.text: Forgot half of it the first time, didn't I! No harm — the wardens never tire of teaching. Better to leave this shore certain than reach the mainland guessing.

bye.speaker: npc
bye.text: Safe crossing to you, friend. Maybe we'll share a road one day.

greet.speaker: npc
greet.text: Heh. Another young sprout off to make a fortune. I've seen a thousand of you come and go from this very bench. Want an old woman's wisdom, or are you in too much of a hurry?

wisdom.speaker: npc
wisdom.text: Then hear this: the isle gives you tools, but it's the trying that makes you. Chop the tree, miss the fish, burn the shrimp — every blunder here is a lesson the mainland charges dearly for. Fail freely while you can.

hurry.speaker: npc
hurry.text: Ha! They always are. Off you go then — the world won't wait, and neither will I.

bye.speaker: npc
bye.text: Go on now. And mind you don't forget old Maven when you're a famous hero.
